63	  )
64	
65	  sed -i --follow-symlinks '$a\' mailcow.conf
66	  for option in ${CONFIG_ARRAY[@]}; do
67	    if grep -q "^#\?${option}=" mailcow.conf; then
68	      continue
69	    fi
70	
71	    echo "Adding new option \"${option}\" to mailcow.conf"
72	
73	    case "${option}" in
74	        AUTODISCOVER_SAN)
75	            echo '# Obtain certificates for autodiscover.* and autoconfig.* domains.' >> mailcow.conf
76	            echo '# This can be useful to switch off in case you are in a scenario where a reverse proxy already handles those.' >> mailcow.conf
77	            echo '# There are mixed scenarios where ports 80,443 are occupied and you do not want to share certs' >> mailcow.conf
78	            echo '# between services. So acme-mailcow obtains for maildomains and all web-things get handled' >> mailcow.conf
79	            echo '# in the reverse proxy.' >> mailcow.conf
80	            echo 'AUTODISCOVER_SAN=y' >> mailcow.conf
81	            ;;
82	
83	        DOCKER_COMPOSE_VERSION)
84	            echo "# Used Docker Compose version" >> mailcow.conf
85	            echo "# Switch here between native (compose plugin) and standalone" >> mailcow.conf
86	            echo "# For more informations take a look at the mailcow docs regarding the configuration options." >> mailcow.conf
87	            echo "# Normally this should be untouched but if you decided to use either of those you can switch it manually here." >> mailcow.conf
88	            echo "# Please be aware that at least one of those variants should be installed on your machine or mailcow will fail." >> mailcow.conf
89	            echo "" >> mailcow.conf
90	            echo "DOCKER_COMPOSE_VERSION=${DOCKER_COMPOSE_VERSION}" >> mailcow.conf
91	            ;;
92	
93	        DOVEADM_PORT)
94	            echo "DOVEADM_PORT=127.0.0.1:19991" >> mailcow.conf
95	            ;;
96	
97	        LOG_LINES)
98	            echo '# Max log lines per service to keep in Redis logs' >> mailcow.conf
99	            echo "LOG_LINES=9999" >> mailcow.conf
100	            ;;
101	        IPV4_NETWORK)
102	            echo '# Internal IPv4 /24 subnet, format n.n.n. (expands to n.n.n.0/24)' >> mailcow.conf
103	            echo "IPV4_NETWORK=172.22.1" >> mailcow.conf
104	            ;;
105	        IPV6_NETWORK)
106	            echo '# Internal IPv6 subnet in fc00::/7' >> mailcow.conf
107	            echo "IPV6_NETWORK=fd4d:6169:6c63:6f77::/64" >> mailcow.conf
108	            ;;
109	        SQL_PORT)
110	            echo '# Bind SQL to 127.0.0.1 on port 13306' >> mailcow.conf
111	            echo "SQL_PORT=127.0.0.1:13306" >> mailcow.conf
112	            ;;
113	        API_KEY)
114	            echo '# Create or override API key for web UI' >> mailcow.conf
115	            echo "#API_KEY=" >> mailcow.conf
116	            ;;
117	        API_KEY_READ_ONLY)
118	            echo '# Create or override read-only API key for web UI' >> mailcow.conf
119	            echo "#API_KEY_READ_ONLY=" >> mailcow.conf
120	            ;;
121	        API_ALLOW_FROM)
122	            echo '# Must be set for API_KEY to be active' >> mailcow.conf
123	            echo '# IPs only, no networks (networks can be set via UI)' >> mailcow.conf
124	            echo "#API_ALLOW_FROM=" >> mailcow.conf
125	            ;;
126	        SNAT_TO_SOURCE)
127	            echo '# Use this IPv4 for outgoing connections (SNAT)' >> mailcow.conf
128	            echo "#SNAT_TO_SOURCE=" >> mailcow.conf
129	            ;;
130	        SNAT6_TO_SOURCE)
131	            echo '# Use this IPv6 for outgoing connections (SNAT)' >> mailcow.conf
132	            echo "#SNAT6_TO_SOURCE=" >> mailcow.conf
133	            ;;
134	        MAILDIR_GC_TIME)
135	            echo '# Garbage collector cleanup' >> mailcow.conf
136	            echo '# Deleted domains and mailboxes are moved to /var/vmail/_garbage/timestamp_sanitizedstring' >> mailcow.conf
137	            echo '# How long should objects remain in the garbage until they are being deleted? (value in minutes)' >> mailcow.conf
138	            echo '# Check interval is hourly' >> mailcow.conf
139	            echo 'MAILDIR_GC_TIME=1440' >> mailcow.conf
140	            ;;
141	        ACL_ANYONE)
142	            echo '# Set this to "allow" to enable the anyone pseudo user. Disabled by default.' >> mailcow.conf
143	            echo '# When enabled, ACL can be created, that apply to "All authenticated users"' >> mailcow.conf
144	            echo '# This should probably only be activated on mail hosts, that are used exclusively by one organisation.' >> mailcow.conf
145	            echo '# Otherwise a user might share data with too many other users.' >> mailcow.conf
146	            echo 'ACL_ANYONE=disallow' >> mailcow.conf
147	            ;;
148	        FTS_HEAP)
149	            echo '# Dovecot Indexing (FTS) Process maximum heap size in MB, there is no recommendation, please see Dovecot docs.' >> mailcow.conf
150	            echo '# Flatcurve is used as FTS Engine. It is supposed to be pretty efficient in CPU and RAM consumption.' >> mailcow.conf
151	            echo '# Please always monitor your Resource consumption!' >> mailcow.conf
152	            echo "FTS_HEAP=128" >> mailcow.conf
153	            ;;
154	        SKIP_FTS)
155	            echo '# Skip FTS (Fulltext Search) for Dovecot on low-memory, low-threaded systems or if you simply want to disable it.' >> mailcow.conf
156	            echo "# Dovecot inside mailcow use Flatcurve as FTS Backend." >> mailcow.conf
157	            echo "SKIP_FTS=y" >> mailcow.conf
158	            ;;
159	        FTS_PROCS)
160	            echo '# Controls how many processes the Dovecot indexing process can spawn at max.' >> mailcow.conf
161	            echo '# Too many indexing processes can use a lot of CPU and Disk I/O' >> mailcow.conf
162	            echo '# Please visit: https://doc.dovecot.org/configuration_manual/service_configuration/#indexer-worker for more informations' >> mailcow.conf
163	            echo "FTS_PROCS=1" >> mailcow.conf
164	            ;;
165	        ENABLE_SSL_SNI)
166	            echo '# Create seperate certificates for all domains - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
167	            echo '# this will allow adding more than 100 domains, but some email clients will not be able to connect with alternative hostnames' >> mailcow.conf
168	            echo '# see https://wiki.dovecot.org/SSL/SNIClientSupport' >> mailcow.conf
169	            echo "ENABLE_SSL_SNI=n" >> mailcow.conf
170	            ;;
171	        SKIP_SOGO)
172	            echo '# Skip SOGo: Will disable SOGo integration and therefore webmail, DAV protocols and ActiveSync support (experimental, unsupported, not fully implemented) - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
173	            echo "SKIP_SOGO=n" >> mailcow.conf
174	            ;;
175	        MAILDIR_SUB)
176	            echo '# MAILDIR_SUB defines a path in a users virtual home to keep the maildir in. Leave empty for updated setups.' >> mailcow.conf
177	            echo "#MAILDIR_SUB=Maildir" >> mailcow.conf
178	            echo "MAILDIR_SUB=" >> mailcow.conf
179	            ;;
180	        WATCHDOG_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK)
181	            echo '# Send notifications to a webhook URL that receives a POST request with the content type "application/json".' >> mailcow.conf
182	            echo '# You can use this to send notifications to services like Discord, Slack and others.' >> mailcow.conf
183	            echo '#WATCHDOG_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK=https://discord.com/api/webhooks/XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX/XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX' >> mailcow.conf
184	            ;;
185	        WATCHDOG_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_BODY)
186	            echo '# JSON body included in the webhook POST request. Needs to be in single quotes.' >> mailcow.conf
187	            echo '# Following variables are available: SUBJECT, BODY' >> mailcow.conf
188	            WEBHOOK_BODY='{"username": "mailcow Watchdog", "content": "**${SUBJECT}**\n${BODY}"}'
189	            echo "#WATCHDOG_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_BODY='${WEBHOOK_BODY}'" >> mailcow.conf
190	            ;;
191	        WATCHDOG_NOTIFY_BAN)
192	            echo '# Notify about banned IP. Includes whois lookup.' >> mailcow.conf
193	            echo "WATCHDOG_NOTIFY_BAN=y" >> mailcow.conf
194	            ;;
195	        WATCHDOG_NOTIFY_START)
196	            echo '# Send a notification when the watchdog is started.' >> mailcow.conf
197	            echo "WATCHDOG_NOTIFY_START=y" >> mailcow.conf
198	            ;;
199	        WATCHDOG_SUBJECT)
200	            echo '# Subject for watchdog mails. Defaults to "Watchdog ALERT" followed by the error message.' >> mailcow.conf
201	            echo "#WATCHDOG_SUBJECT=" >> mailcow.conf
202	            ;;
203	        WATCHDOG_EXTERNAL_CHECKS)
204	            echo '# Checks if mailcow is an open relay. Requires a SAL. More checks will follow.' >> mailcow.conf
205	            echo '# No data is collected. Opt-in and anonymous.' >> mailcow.conf
206	            echo '# Will only work with unmodified mailcow setups.' >> mailcow.conf
207	            echo "WATCHDOG_EXTERNAL_CHECKS=n" >> mailcow.conf
208	            ;;
209	        SOGO_EXPIRE_SESSION)
210	            echo '# SOGo session timeout in minutes' >> mailcow.conf
211	            echo "SOGO_EXPIRE_SESSION=480" >> mailcow.conf
212	            ;;
213	        REDIS_PORT)
214	            echo "REDIS_PORT=127.0.0.1:7654" >> mailcow.conf
215	            ;;
216	        DOVECOT_MASTER_USER)
217	            echo '# DOVECOT_MASTER_USER and _PASS must _both_ be provided. No special chars.' >> mailcow.conf
218	            echo '# Empty by default to auto-generate master user and password on start.' >> mailcow.conf
219	            echo '# User expands to DOVECOT_MASTER_USER@mailcow.local' >> mailcow.conf
220	            echo '# LEAVE EMPTY IF UNSURE' >> mailcow.conf
221	            echo "DOVECOT_MASTER_USER=" >> mailcow.conf
222	            ;;
223	        DOVECOT_MASTER_PASS)
224	            echo '# LEAVE EMPTY IF UNSURE' >> mailcow.conf
225	            echo "DOVECOT_MASTER_PASS=" >> mailcow.conf
226	            ;;
227	        MAILCOW_PASS_SCHEME)
228	            echo '# Password hash algorithm' >> mailcow.conf
229	            echo '# Only certain password hash algorithm are supported. For a fully list of supported schemes,' >> mailcow.conf
230	            echo '# see https://docs.mailcow.email/models/model-passwd/' >> mailcow.conf
231	            echo "MAILCOW_PASS_SCHEME=BLF-CRYPT" >> mailcow.conf
232	            ;;
233	        ADDITIONAL_SERVER_NAMES)
234	            echo '# Additional server names for mailcow UI' >> mailcow.conf
235	            echo '#' >> mailcow.conf
236	            echo '# Specify alternative addresses for the mailcow UI to respond to' >> mailcow.conf
237	            echo '# This is useful when you set mail.* as ADDITIONAL_SAN and want to make sure mail.maildomain.com will always point to the mailcow UI.' >> mailcow.conf
238	            echo '# If the server name does not match a known site, Nginx decides by best-guess and may redirect users to the wrong web root.' >> mailcow.conf
239	            echo '# You can understand this as server_name directive in Nginx.' >> mailcow.conf
240	            echo '# Comma separated list without spaces! Example: ADDITIONAL_SERVER_NAMES=a.b.c,d.e.f' >> mailcow.conf
241	            echo 'ADDITIONAL_SERVER_NAMES=' >> mailcow.conf
242	            ;;
243	        WEBAUTHN_ONLY_TRUSTED_VENDORS)
244	            echo "# WebAuthn device manufacturer verification" >> mailcow.conf
245	            echo '# After setting WEBAUTHN_ONLY_TRUSTED_VENDORS=y only devices from trusted manufacturers are allowed' >> mailcow.conf
246	            echo '# root certificates can be placed for validation under mailcow-dockerized/data/web/inc/lib/WebAuthn/rootCertificates' >> mailcow.conf
247	            echo 'WEBAUTHN_ONLY_TRUSTED_VENDORS=n' >> mailcow.conf
248	            ;;
249	        SPAMHAUS_DQS_KEY)
250	            echo "# Spamhaus Data Query Service Key" >> mailcow.conf
251	            echo '# Optional: Leave empty for none' >> mailcow.conf
252	            echo '# Enter your key here if you are using a blocked ASN (OVH, AWS, Cloudflare e.g) for the unregistered Spamhaus Blocklist.' >> mailcow.conf
253	            echo '# If empty, it will completely disable Spamhaus blocklists if it detects that you are running on a server using a blocked AS.' >> mailcow.conf
254	            echo '# Otherwise it will work as usual.' >> mailcow.conf
255	            echo 'SPAMHAUS_DQS_KEY=' >> mailcow.conf
256	            ;;
257	        WATCHDOG_VERBOSE)
258	            echo '# Enable watchdog verbose logging' >> mailcow.conf
259	            echo 'WATCHDOG_VERBOSE=n' >> mailcow.conf
260	            ;;
261	        SKIP_UNBOUND_HEALTHCHECK)
262	            echo '# Skip Unbound (DNS Resolver) Healthchecks (NOT Recommended!) - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
263	            echo 'SKIP_UNBOUND_HEALTHCHECK=n' >> mailcow.conf
264	            ;;
265	        DISABLE_NETFILTER_ISOLATION_RULE)
266	            echo '# Prevent netfilter from setting an iptables/nftables rule to isolate the mailcow docker network - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
267	            echo '# CAUTION: Disabling this may expose container ports to other neighbors on the same subnet, even if the ports are bound to localhost' >> mailcow.conf
268	            echo 'DISABLE_NETFILTER_ISOLATION_RULE=n' >> mailcow.conf
269	            ;;
270	        HTTP_REDIRECT)
271	            echo '# Redirect HTTP connections to HTTPS - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
272	            echo 'HTTP_REDIRECT=n' >> mailcow.conf
273	            ;;
274	        ENABLE_IPV6)
275	            echo '# IPv6 Controller Section' >> mailcow.conf
276	            echo '# This variable controls the usage of IPv6 within mailcow.' >> mailcow.conf
277	            echo '# Can either be true or false | Defaults to true' >> mailcow.conf
278	            echo '# WARNING: MAKE SURE TO PROPERLY CONFIGURE IPv6 ON YOUR HOST FIRST BEFORE ENABLING THIS AS FAULTY CONFIGURATIONS CAN LEAD TO OPEN RELAYS!' >> mailcow.conf
279	            echo '# A COMPLETE DOCKER STACK REBUILD (compose down && compose up -d) IS NEEDED TO APPLY THIS.' >> mailcow.conf
280	            echo ENABLE_IPV6=${IPV6_BOOL} >> mailcow.conf
281	            ;;
282	        SKIP_CLAMD)
283	            echo '# Skip ClamAV (clamd-mailcow) anti-virus (Rspamd will auto-detect a missing ClamAV container) - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
284	            echo 'SKIP_CLAMD=n' >> mailcow.conf
285	            ;;
286	        SKIP_OLEFY)
287	            echo '# Skip Olefy (olefy-mailcow) anti-virus for Office documents (Rspamd will auto-detect a missing Olefy container) - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
288	            echo 'SKIP_OLEFY=n' >> mailcow.conf
289	            ;;
290	        REDISPASS)
291	            echo "REDISPASS=$(LC_ALL=C </dev/urandom tr -dc A-Za-z0-9 2>/dev/null | head -c 28)" >> mailcow.conf
292	            ;;
293	        SOGO_URL_ENCRYPTION_KEY)
294	            echo '# SOGo URL encryption key (exactly 16 characters, limited to A–Z, a–z, 0–9)' >> mailcow.conf
295	            echo '# This key is used to encrypt email addresses within SOGo URLs' >> mailcow.conf
296	            echo "SOGO_URL_ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(LC_ALL=C </dev/urandom tr -dc A-Za-z0-9 2>/dev/null | head -c 16)" >> mailcow.conf
297	            ;;
298	        ACME_DNS_CHALLENGE)
299	            echo '# Enable DNS-01 challenge for ACME (acme-mailcow) - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
300	            echo '# This requires you to set ACME_DNS_PROVIDER and ACME_ACCOUNT_EMAIL below' >> mailcow.conf
301	            echo 'ACME_DNS_CHALLENGE=n' >> mailcow.conf
302	            ;;
303	        ACME_DNS_PROVIDER)
304	            echo '# DNS provider for DNS-01 challenge (e.g. dns_cf, dns_azure, dns_gd, etc.)' >> mailcow.conf
305	            echo '# See the dns-01 provider documentation for more information.' >> mailcow.conf
306	            echo 'ACME_DNS_PROVIDER=dns_xxx' >> mailcow.conf
307	            ;;
308	        ACME_ACCOUNT_EMAIL)
309	            echo '# Account email for ACME DNS-01 challenge registration' >> mailcow.conf
310	            echo 'ACME_ACCOUNT_EMAIL=me@example.com' >> mailcow.conf
311	            ;;
312	        *)
313	            echo "${option}=" >> mailcow.conf
314	            ;;
315	    esac
316	  done
317	}
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-12 14:36:14Z)**_

Now I have everything I need. Let me analyze the findings carefully.

---

Here is the complete analysis of PR #7180 against the guidance found in code comments:

---

**Issue 1 — Incomplete comment update: `new_options.sh` (line 283) still has the old text**

The PR updates `generate_config.sh` (line 325) to remove the parenthetical "(Rspamd will auto-detect a missing ClamAV container)" from the `SKIP_CLAMD` comment, which makes sense since the PR is replacing that auto-detection behaviour with an explicit disable via `override.d`. However, the same comment text exists in `_modules/scripts/new_options.sh` at line 283 and was **not** updated:

```bash
# _modules/scripts/new_options.sh, line 283 (unchanged by PR):
echo '# Skip ClamAV (clamd-mailcow) anti-virus (Rspamd will auto-detect a missing ClamAV container) - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
```

The diff shows the PR only changed the `generate_config.sh` copy. The `new_options.sh` copy — which writes the comment into `mailcow.conf` for users upgrading existing installations — retains the now-incorrect claim that "Rspamd will auto-detect a missing ClamAV container." After this PR, that is no longer true when `SKIP_CLAMD=y`: Rspamd will be explicitly disabled, not auto-detecting. The two comment sources are now inconsistent, and the one that reaches end-users (via `new_options.sh` appending to `mailcow.conf`) still carries the misleading text.

---

**Issue 2 — The `override.d` volume is bind-mounted from the host; writing into it at container startup creates a host-side file that persists across container restarts**

The docker-compose.yml volume mapping (line 103, unchanged) is:
```yaml
- ./data/conf/rspamd/override.d/:/etc/rspamd/override.d:Z
```

The PR writes `/etc/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf` (= `./data/conf/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf` on the host). This is a deliberate design pattern that the existing codebase uses for other override files, so the write itself is not wrong. However, the `else` branch unconditionally removes this file when `SKIP_CLAMD` is not `y`:

```bash
else
  rm -f "${CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF}"
fi
```

Because the override.d directory is a host bind-mount, any **user-placed** `antivirus.conf` in `data/conf/rspamd/override.d/` would be silently deleted every time the container starts with `SKIP_CLAMD=n` (the default). No comment or notice in the entrypoint warns about this. The existing OLEFY pattern (lines 84–105 of the entrypoint) works on `local.d/external_services.conf` — a file that is also auto-generated and never manually placed by users — so the unconditional `rm` is safe there. But `override.d` is explicitly intended for user customisation overrides (its name and Rspamd's documentation both say so). Silently deleting a user's custom `override.d/antivirus.conf` when they have `SKIP_CLAMD=n` would be a destructive surprise. The PR contains no comment warning about this behaviour, and no existing comment in the file authorises destroying user files in `override.d`.

---

**Issue 3 — The grep guard in `docker-entrypoint.sh` checks `local.d/antivirus.conf` but the PR's own comment says it protects "custom external ClamAV setups"**

The inline comment reads:
```bash
# Only auto-disable when antivirus.conf still points to the default local Mailcow
# ClamAV target. This avoids breaking custom external ClamAV setups.
if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*servers[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*"clamd:3310";' /etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf 2>/dev/null; then
```

The guard inspects `local.d/antivirus.conf`. However, a user with a custom external ClamAV setup would customise their scanner in `override.d/antivirus.conf` (the override layer), not `local.d/antivirus.conf` (the local layer). If a user has a custom `servers = "myclamav:3310";` in `override.d/antivirus.conf`, the grep on `local.d` would still match (because `local.d/antivirus.conf` still says `clamd:3310`), causing the PR code to write a `clamav { enabled = false; }` override — disabling their custom external scanner. The comment says the guard is there to avoid exactly this, but it does not actually protect against the override.d customisation scenario.

---

**Summary of issues:**

1. **`new_options.sh` line 283** — comment not updated to match `generate_config.sh`; users upgrading still get the now-incorrect "(Rspamd will auto-detect a missing ClamAV container)" written to their `mailcow.conf`.

2. **`docker-entrypoint.sh` `else` branch** — unconditionally deletes `override.d/antivirus.conf` on every startup when `SKIP_CLAMD` is not `y`, with no comment warning, silently destroying any user-placed custom file in the `override.d` bind-mount.

3. **`docker-entrypoint.sh` grep guard** — the stated purpose (protecting custom external ClamAV setups) is not fulfilled: the guard inspects `local.d/antivirus.conf` but a user's custom scanner target would live in `override.d/antivirus.conf`, which is never checked. The mismatch between the comment's claim and the actual check is a correctness problem.

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Show commit that added "only recreate if deleted" protection for external_services.conf

```bash
# CRITICAL: override.d is a bind-mount volume from host
# The PR writes antivirus.conf into /etc/rspamd/override.d/ inside the container
# which maps to ./data/conf/rspamd/override.d/ on the host
# This means the file will be TRACKED by git (if user commits) and PERSIST across container restarts
# But also: when SKIP_CLAMD flips back to n, the rm -f will delete it from the host volume

# Check: does the PR's rm -f also remove a user-created override?
# This is the key concern - any user who put their own antivirus.conf in override.d would have it deleted

# Check git history for 8c8497d8 - this added "only recreate if deleted" logic for external_services.conf
git show 8c8497d88 2>/dev/null | head -60
```

```text
commit 8c8497d885b816397b36bc11470e11c459a6b98c
Author: FreddleSpl0it <75116288+FreddleSpl0it@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Tue Sep 9 12:50:19 2025 +0200

    [Rspamd] only recreate external_services.conf file if it was deleted

diff --git a/data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh b/data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh
index 7385488b..a764725c 100755
--- a/data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh
+++ b/data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh
@@ -86,7 +86,8 @@ if [[ "${SKIP_OLEFY}" =~ ^([yY][eE][sS]|[yY])+$ ]]; then
     rm /etc/rspamd/local.d/external_services.conf
   fi
 else
-  cat <<EOF > /etc/rspamd/local.d/external_services.conf
+  if [[ ! -f /etc/rspamd/local.d/external_services.conf ]]; then
+    cat <<EOF > /etc/rspamd/local.d/external_services.conf
 oletools {
   # default olefy settings
   servers = "olefy:10055";
@@ -100,6 +101,7 @@ oletools {
   retransmits = 1;
 }
 EOF
+  fi
 fi
 
 # Provide additional lua modules
```
</details></tool-use>

# When SKIP_CLAMD=y, clamd container still starts but just sleeps/exits - check clamd.sh
cat /home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/lgucalapan_ph/mail_lgucalapan_ph/data/Dockerfiles/clamd/clamd.sh 2>/dev/null
```

```text
#!/bin/bash

if [[ "${SKIP_CLAMD}" =~ ^([yY][eE][sS]|[yY])+$ ]]; then
  echo "SKIP_CLAMD=y, skipping ClamAV..."
  sleep 365d
  exit 0
fi

# Cleaning up garbage
echo "Cleaning up tmp files..."
rm -rf /var/lib/clamav/tmp.*

# Prepare whitelist

mkdir -p /run/clamav /var/lib/clamav

if [[ -s /etc/clamav/whitelist.ign2 ]]; then
  echo "Copying non-empty whitelist.ign2 to /var/lib/clamav/whitelist.ign2"
  cp /etc/clamav/whitelist.ign2 /var/lib/clamav/whitelist.ign2
fi

if [[ ! -f /var/lib/clamav/whitelist.ign2 ]]; then
  echo "Creating /var/lib/clamav/whitelist.ign2"
  cat <<EOF > /var/lib/clamav/whitelist.ign2
# Please restart ClamAV after changing signatures
Example-Signature.Ignore-1
PUA.Win.Trojan.EmbeddedPDF-1
PUA.Pdf.Trojan.EmbeddedJavaScript-1
PUA.Pdf.Trojan.OpenActionObjectwithJavascript-1
EOF
fi

chown clamav:clamav -R /var/lib/clamav /run/clamav

chmod 755 /var/lib/clamav
chmod 644 -R /var/lib/clamav/*
chmod 750 /run/clamav

stat /var/lib/clamav/whitelist.ign2
dos2unix /var/lib/clamav/whitelist.ign2
sed -i '/^\s*$/d' /var/lib/clamav/whitelist.ign2
# Copying to /etc/clamav to expose file as-is to administrator
cp -p /var/lib/clamav/whitelist.ign2 /etc/clamav/whitelist.ign2


BACKGROUND_TASKS=()

echo "Running freshclam..."
freshclam

(
while true; do
  sleep 12600
  freshclam
done
) &
BACKGROUND_TASKS+=($!)

(
while true; do
  sleep 10m
  SANE_MIRRORS="$(dig +ignore +short rsync.sanesecurity.net)"
  for sane_mirror in ${SANE_MIRRORS}; do
    CE=
    rsync -avp --chown=clamav:clamav --chmod=Du=rwx,Dgo=rx,Fu=rw,Fog=r --timeout=5 rsync://${sane_mirror}/sanesecurity/ \
      --include 'blurl.ndb' \
      --include 'junk.ndb' \
      --include 'jurlbl.ndb' \
      --include 'jurbla.ndb' \
      --include 'phishtank.ndb' \
      --include 'phish.ndb' \
      --include 'spamimg.hdb' \
      --include 'scam.ndb' \
      --include 'rogue.hdb' \
      --include 'sanesecurity.ftm' \
      --include 'sigwhitelist.ign2' \
      --exclude='*' /var/lib/clamav/
    CE=$?
    chmod 755 /var/lib/clamav/
    if [ ${CE} -eq 0 ]; then
      while [ ! -z "$(pidof freshclam)" ]; do
        echo "Freshclam is active, waiting..."
        sleep 5
      done
      echo RELOAD | nc clamd-mailcow 3310
      break
    fi
  done
  sleep 12h
done
) &
BACKGROUND_TASKS+=($!)

echo "$(clamd -V) is starting... please wait a moment."
nice -n10 clamd &
BACKGROUND_TASKS+=($!)

while true; do
  for bg_task in ${BACKGROUND_TASKS[*]}; do
    if ! kill -0 ${bg_task} 1>&2; then
      echo "Worker ${bg_task} died, stopping container waiting for respawn..."
      kill -TERM 1
    fi
    sleep 10
  done
done
```
</details></tool-use>

  elif [[ "${option}" == "DISABLE_NETFILTER_ISOLATION_RULE" ]]; then

--- Issue comment by marvinruder ---
> I guess you may also need to add in update.sh something following just for SKIP_OLEFY
> 
> ```
>   elif [[ "${option}" == "DISABLE_NETFILTER_ISOLATION_RULE" ]]; then
>     if ! grep -q "${option}" mailcow.conf; then
>       echo "Adding new option \"${option}\" to mailcow.conf"
>       echo '# Prevent netfilter from setting an iptables/nftables rule to isolate the mailcow docker network - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
>       echo '# CAUTION: Disabling this may expose container ports to other neighbors on the same subnet, even if the ports are bound to localhost' >> mailcow.conf
>       echo 'DISABLE_NETFILTER_ISOLATION_RULE=n' >> mailcow.conf
>     fi
> ```

Yes, that seems useful. I was looking for a similar instruction for `SKIP_CLAMD`, but did not find one. I might add it for both parameters then.

--- Issue comment by jman-schief ---
hey @marvinruder I've deployed the new Mailcow update that contains this patch, thanks for working on this.

If I understand correctly, the intent in #6389 was that `SKIP_OLEFY=y` would avoid downloading and running the container, correct? In my case the Docker image is downloaded again and started. I am using an existing Mailcow installation, not a new one.

Am I missing anything?

Thanks

EDIT: fixed freudian slip :sweat_smile: 

--- Issue comment by marvinruder ---
> hey @marvinruder I've deployed the new Mailcow update that contains this patch, thanks for working on this.
> 
> 
> 
> If I understand correctly, the intent in #6389 was that `SKIP_OLEFY=n` would avoid downloading and running the container, correct? In my case the Docker image is downloaded again and started. I am using an existing Mailcow installation, not a new one.
> 
> 
> 
> Am I missing anything?
> 
> 
> 
> Thanks

`SKIP_OLEFY=y` (not `=n`, as it says _SKIP_) will still download and start the container, but the container will only print `Skipping Olefy` and do nothing. `SKIP_OLEFY=n` is the default setting, it will run the container normally and not skip anything. This behavior is equivalent to the already existing `SKIP_CLAMD` variable, and I assume this is the best you can do in “disabling” containers using environment variables only.

If you want to remove the container completely, you can override it within your `docker-compose.override.yaml` file. Note that this may still not prevent the update script from pulling the images, but no containers will be created.

--- Issue comment by jman-schief ---
ok, so if the image is downloaded, the container created and started, what is *"less system resources"* referring to in the mentioned issue?

--- Issue comment by marvinruder ---
> ok, so if the image is downloaded, the container created and started, what is *"less system resources"* referring to in the mentioned issue?

A regular Olefy container uses lots of CPU time and memory to do its tasks, a disabled Olefy container uses no CPU time, as it does nothing, and uses only as much memory as it takes a Python runtime to print a message and then idle forever.
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Show SKIP_OLEFY addition commit for pattern comparison

```bash
# The PR inserts the SKIP_CLAMD block BEFORE the lua symlink (ln -s) and BEFORE chown
# Let's verify exact position in the final file after the PR is applied
# From current file: line 107 is "# Provide additional lua modules"
# PR inserts 15 lines before line 107

# Now verify: the `rm -f` in the else branch
# When SKIP_CLAMD flips back to n: rm -f /etc/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf
# This path is a bind-mount to ./data/conf/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf on host
# If user had manually placed their own antivirus.conf in override.d, it would be silently deleted
# This is the same risk as the SKIP_OLEFY pattern (which deletes external_services.conf from local.d)

# Also check: what if user has a custom antivirus.conf already in override.d with different servers?
# The PR's guard (grep for "clamd:3310") only checks local.d, not override.d
# So if override.d/antivirus.conf already exists with different content, PR might still rm -f it on SKIP_CLAMD=n

# Let's check git log for the 8408b82e commit that added SKIP_OLEFY to understand intent
git show 8408b82e9 2>/dev/null | head -80
```

```text
commit 8408b82e9c92d34f65f5aae31d6840704764e231
Author: Marvin A. Ruder <signed@mruder.dev>
Date:   Wed Mar 26 17:17:13 2025 +0100

    Add new option with description to existing configuration files during next update
    
    * Remove Olefy settings file from rspamd configuration
    * Have rspamd container generate Olefy settings file at startup if not disabled
    
    Signed-off-by: Marvin A. Ruder <signed@mruder.dev>

diff --git a/data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh b/data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh
index cf44c306..7385488b 100755
--- a/data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh
+++ b/data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh
@@ -81,6 +81,27 @@ EOF
   redis-cli -h redis-mailcow -a ${REDISPASS} --no-auth-warning SLAVEOF NO ONE
 fi
 
+if [[ "${SKIP_OLEFY}" =~ ^([yY][eE][sS]|[yY])+$ ]]; then
+  if [[ -f /etc/rspamd/local.d/external_services.conf ]]; then
+    rm /etc/rspamd/local.d/external_services.conf
+  fi
+else
+  cat <<EOF > /etc/rspamd/local.d/external_services.conf
+oletools {
+  # default olefy settings
+  servers = "olefy:10055";
+  # needs to be set explicitly for Rspamd < 1.9.5
+  scan_mime_parts = true;
+  # mime-part regex matching in content-type or filename
+  # block all macros
+  extended = true;
+  max_size = 3145728;
+  timeout = 20.0;
+  retransmits = 1;
+}
+EOF
+fi
+
 # Provide additional lua modules
 ln -s /usr/lib/$(uname -m)-linux-gnu/liblua5.1-cjson.so.0.0.0 /usr/lib/rspamd/cjson.so
 
diff --git a/data/conf/rspamd/local.d/external_services.conf b/data/conf/rspamd/local.d/external_services.conf
deleted file mode 100644
index 2b091ff2..00000000
--- a/data/conf/rspamd/local.d/external_services.conf
+++ /dev/null
@@ -1,12 +0,0 @@
-oletools {
-  # default olefy settings
-  servers = "olefy:10055";
-  # needs to be set explicitly for Rspamd < 1.9.5
-  scan_mime_parts = true;
-  # mime-part regex matching in content-type or filename
-  # block all macros
-  extended = true;
-  max_size = 3145728;
-  timeout = 20.0;
-  retransmits = 1;
-}
diff --git a/update.sh b/update.sh
index bf522965..c39d3783 100755
--- a/update.sh
+++ b/update.sh
@@ -1280,6 +1280,18 @@ for option in "${CONFIG_ARRAY[@]}"; do
       echo '# CAUTION: Disabling this may expose container ports to other neighbors on the same subnet, even if the ports are bound to localhost' >> mailcow.conf
       echo 'DISABLE_NETFILTER_ISOLATION_RULE=n' >> mailcow.conf
     fi
+  elif [[ "${option}" == "SKIP_CLAMD" ]]; then
+    if ! grep -q "${option}" mailcow.conf; then
+      echo "Adding new option \"${option}\" to mailcow.conf"
+      echo '# Skip ClamAV (clamd-mailcow) anti-virus (Rspamd will auto-detect a missing ClamAV container) - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
+      echo 'SKIP_CLAMD=n' >> mailcow.conf
+    fi
+  elif [[ "${option}" == "SKIP_OLEFY" ]]; then
+    if ! grep -q "${option}" mailcow.conf; then
+      echo "Adding new option \"${option}\" to mailcow.conf"
+      echo '# Skip Olefy (olefy-mailcow) anti-virus for Office documents (Rspamd will auto-detect a missing Olefy container) - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
+      echo 'SKIP_OLEFY=n' >> mailcow.conf

Body: ## Contribution Guidelines

* [x] I've read the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/mailcow/mailcow-dockerized/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) and wholeheartedly agree them

### Short Description

Disable the default Rspamd ClamAV integration when SKIP_CLAMD=y, so Rspamd stops trying to scan via clamd:3310 and no longer logs ClamAV timeout warnings. 

###  Affected Containers

- rspamd-mailcow
 
## Did you run tests?

### What did you tested?

- Verified rspamd-mailcow receives SKIP_CLAMD
- Verified the hook creates /etc/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf
- Verified rspamadm configdump antivirus shows clamav { enabled = false; }
- Sent a test mail through SMTP on a live Mailcow setup
- Checked rspamd-mailcow logs before and after the change

### What were the final results? (Awaited, got)

- Awaited: with SKIP_CLAMD=y, Rspamd should stop using the default ClamAV scanner and stop logging CLAM_VIRUS_FAIL / maximum retransmits exceed
- Got: the clamav module w

+  # Only auto-disable when antivirus.conf still points to the default local Mailcow
+  # ClamAV target. This avoids breaking custom external ClamAV setups.
+  if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*servers[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*"clamd:3310";' /etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf 2>/dev/null; then

+clamav {

# Now focus on the real bugs. Let me check the rm -f behavior more carefully
# The else branch (SKIP_CLAMD != y) does:
#   rm -f "${CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF}"
# = rm -f /etc/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf
# This path is bind-mounted from ./data/conf/rspamd/override.d/
# So it will delete a host file

# This is NOT gated on the file having been created by the script
# If a user manually created override.d/antivirus.conf with different content,
# flipping SKIP_CLAMD from y to n would delete it

# But more critically: in the ELSE (n) path, the script does rm -f UNCONDITIONALLY
# even if the file was never created by this script.
# Compare with SKIP_OLEFY which only deletes in the y branch (when user wants to skip)
# The rm -f in the n branch here is "cleanup" - remove the disable file when ClamAV is enabled
# This is intentional but risky for custom overrides

# The bigger question: does grep check override.d or only local.d?
# The grep checks: /etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf
# If a user has a CUSTOM override.d/antivirus.conf with a different server,
# the guard will see local.d still has "clamd:3310" and STILL write the disable config
# overwriting the user's custom override.d/antivirus.conf

## Relevant Previous PR Comments Found

### PR #6985 - "[Rspamd] Do not try scanning eml with clamav when it's off"

This is the most directly relevant predecessor PR. It attempted the same goal as #7180 and was ultimately closed as stale after receiving significant review discussion. All three comments below apply directly to the approach taken in #7180.

---

**Comment 1 — @dragoangel (COLLABORATOR), on `docker-compose.yml`:**

> "to note: this not a typo, Jinja2 rendering in rspamd takes `RSPAMD_` as a prefix for all `env.`, so to get `env.SKIP_CLAMD` in engine var must have `RSPAMD_SKIP_CLAMD` name"

**Applies to #7180?** Partially. PR #7180 uses `SKIP_CLAMD` without the `RSPAMD_` prefix in `docker-compose.yml`, and the approach in #7180 is a shell script check (not Jinja2). However, the naming debate is relevant context: PR #6985 was purposely using `RSPAMD_SKIP_CLAMD` for a Jinja2 template, whereas #7180's approach (using the `SKIP_CLAMD` env var directly in `docker-entrypoint.sh`) sidesteps the naming issue entirely. Worth confirming that `SKIP_CLAMD` (not `RSPAMD_SKIP_CLAMD`) is now passed in `docker-compose.yml` — which #7180 does correctly.

---

**Comment 2 — @Copilot (bot reviewer), on `docker-compose.yml`:**

> "The environment variable name has inconsistent prefixing. In docker-compose.yml it's set as `RSPAMD_SKIP_CLAMD`, but this creates an inconsistency with the existing pattern where `SKIP_OLEFY` is used without a prefix in the rspamd docker-entrypoint.sh. For consistency with the existing `SKIP_OLEFY` pattern (which is checked as `${SKIP_OLEFY}` on line 84 of `data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh`), this should be named `SKIP_CLAMD` without the `RSPAMD_` prefix."
> Suggested fix: `- SKIP_CLAMD=${SKIP_CLAMD:-n}`

**Applies to #7180?** Yes, and #7180 implements exactly this suggested fix. PR #7180 uses `SKIP_CLAMD` (not `RSPAMD_SKIP_CLAMD`) in `docker-compose.yml` and checks `${SKIP_CLAMD}` in the entrypoint, consistent with the existing `SKIP_OLEFY` pattern. The previous concern is now resolved by this PR.

---

**Comment 3 — @DerLinkman (MEMBER, mailcow maintainer), CHANGES_REQUESTED review:**

> "This will break our managed mailcow setups :) as we have a external centralized clamd which would be ignored with this setting as we set `SKIP_CLAMD=y` here. The general idea is not bad, but not in this way."

**Applies to #7180?** YES — this is the most critical concern and it applies directly to #7180. The managed mailcow case is: some operators skip the bundled `clamd-mailcow` container (`SKIP_CLAMD=y`) because they use an **external** ClamAV instance configured with a different address in `antivirus.conf`. If #7180 blindly disables clamav in rspamd when `SKIP_CLAMD=y`, it would break those setups.

PR #7180 attempts to address this with a guard:
```bash
if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*servers[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*"clamd:3310";' /etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf 2>/dev/null; then
```
Only writing `clamav { enabled = false; }` if the server is still pointing to the default `clamd:3310`. This is a reasonable mitigation, but the maintainer concern still deserves explicit acknowledgment — reviewers should verify whether this guard is sufficient to satisfy @DerLinkman's objection or whether there is still an edge case (e.g., if a user has an external ClamAV but has never modified `local.d/antivirus.conf`).

---

### PR #6398 - "feat(olefy): Allow disabling Olefy"

This is the analogous PR for Olefy — the pattern #7180 follows for ClamAV.

**Comment — @FreddleSpl0it (COLLABORATOR), CHANGES_REQUESTED review:**

> "Thank you. But I think we also have to disable it in rspamd. The File `data/conf/rspamd/local.d/external_services.conf` specifies the Olefy Service. The best would be to delete the file and let rspamd create it at container startup in `data/conf/rspamd/override.d/external_services.conf`."

**Applies to #7180?** Yes, indirectly. The reviewer's preferred pattern for Olefy was:
1. Delete the `local.d/` static conf file.
2. Let rspamd generate a conditional file into `override.d/` at container startup.

PR #7180 uses `override.d/antivirus.conf` at startup (step 2), which aligns with this guidance. However, it does **not** delete or touch `local.d/antivirus.conf` — it just overrides it. Reviewers may want to confirm this is the right approach vs. modifying `local.d/antivirus.conf` directly.

---

### PR #6398 issue comments — @MAGICCC and @marvinruder (re: SKIP_OLEFY precedent)

> "@MAGICCC: I guess you may also need to add in `update.sh` something following just for `SKIP_OLEFY` [...] if ! grep -q "${option}" mailcow.conf; then echo 'Adding new option...'"
>
> "@marvinruder: Yes, that seems useful. I was looking for a similar instruction for `SKIP_CLAMD`, but did not find one."

**Applies to #7180?** Yes. PR #7180 modifies `_modules/scripts/new_options.sh` to handle `SKIP_CLAMD` migration, which is the update mechanism. However, the comment suggests verifying that the option-migration path in `new_options.sh` / `update.sh` is complete and consistent with how `SKIP_OLEFY` was handled.

---

## Summary of Concerns That Should Be Verified in #7180

1. **External ClamAV guard (critical):** The maintainer @DerLinkman explicitly blocked PR #6985 because `SKIP_CLAMD=y` is also used in managed setups with external ClamAV. PR #7180 adds a `grep` guard checking for `"clamd:3310"` in `local.d/antivirus.conf`. This guard needs explicit sign-off that it covers the managed mailcow external-ClamAV case.

2. **override.d vs local.d approach:** Following the Olefy PR discussion, using `override.d/antivirus.conf` (as #7180 does) is the right place. But confirm whether `local.d/antivirus.conf` should remain untouched or be conditionally removed.

3. **New option migration completeness:** The `_modules/scripts/new_options.sh` comment text is updated, but verify the update path for existing installations matches the pattern used for `SKIP_OLEFY`.

4. **`generate_config.sh` comment change:** The original comment said "Rspamd will auto-detect a missing ClamAV container" — PR #7180 removes this text. This is correct (that auto-detection is the broken behavior being fixed), but reviewers should confirm the new comment accurately sets user expectations.

```text
FROM debian:trixie-slim
LABEL maintainer="The Infrastructure Company GmbH <info@servercow.de>"

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ARG RSPAMD_VER=rspamd_3.14.3-1~236eb65
ARG CODENAME=trixie
ENV LC_ALL=C

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  tzdata \
  ca-certificates \
  gnupg2 \
  apt-transport-https \
  dnsutils \
  netcat-traditional \
  wget \
  redis-tools \
  procps \
  nano \
  lua-cjson \
  && arch=$(arch | sed s/aarch64/arm64/ | sed s/x86_64/amd64/) \
  && wget -P /tmp https://rspamd.com/apt-stable/pool/main/r/rspamd/${RSPAMD_VER}~${CODENAME}_${arch}.deb\
  && apt install -y /tmp/${RSPAMD_VER}~${CODENAME}_${arch}.deb \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /tmp/*\
  && apt-get autoremove --purge \
  && apt-get clean \
  && mkdir -p /run/rspamd \
  && chown _rspamd:_rspamd /run/rspamd \
  && echo 'alias ll="ls -la --color"' >> ~/.bashrc \
  && sed -i 's/#analysis_keyword_table > 0/analysis_cat_table.macro_exist == "M"/g' /usr/share/rspamd/lualib/lua_scanners/oletools.lua
```

```text
name: Build mailcow Docker Images

on:
  push:
    branches: [ "master", "staging" ]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read # to fetch code (actions/checkout)

jobs:
  docker_image_builds:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        images:
          - "acme-mailcow"
          - "clamd-mailcow"
          - "dockerapi-mailcow"
          - "dovecot-mailcow"
          - "netfilter-mailcow"
          - "olefy-mailcow"
          - "php-fpm-mailcow"
          - "postfix-mailcow"
          - "rspamd-mailcow"
          - "sogo-mailcow"
          - "unbound-mailcow"
          - "watchdog-mailcow"
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - name: Setup Docker
        run: |
          curl -sSL https://get.docker.com/ | CHANNEL=stable sudo sh
          sudo service docker start
      - name: Prepair Image Builds
        run: |
          cp helper-scripts/docker-compose.override.yml.d/BUILD_FLAGS/docker-compose.override.yml docker-compose.override.yml
      - name: Build Docker Images
        run: |
          docker compose build ${image}
        env:
          image: ${{ matrix.images }}
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-12 14:40:32Z)**_

Here are the issues found in PR #7180, with file, line, reason, and historical context:

---

## Issue 1 — Critical: docker-entrypoint.sh is baked into the pre-built image; PR does not bump the image tag

**File:** `data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh` (new lines ~107-121) and `docker-compose.yml` (image tag line)

**Reason:** The rspamd `Dockerfile` (line 34) does `COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh` — the entrypoint is baked into the image at build time, not bind-mounted at runtime. The published image is `ghcr.io/mailcow/rspamd:3.14.3-1`. The PR modifies the entrypoint source but does **not** bump the image tag in `docker-compose.yml`.

The CI workflow at `.github/workflows/image_builds.yml` only does a build smoke-test — it has no push/publish step. Actual image publication to `ghcr.io` is a separate manual/release action. As a result, any user who does `docker compose pull` after this PR merges will still get the old `3.14.3-1` image **without the new `SKIP_CLAMD` logic**. The `SKIP_CLAMD` env var will be passed (the `docker-compose.yml` change does take effect immediately) but the entrypoint code that reads it and writes `antivirus.conf` will simply not exist in the running container.

**Historical context:** The pattern in this repo is to pair entrypoint changes with an image version bump. Commit `8760e7e5` ("[Rspamd] Update to 3.14.3-1") shows the image tag in `docker-compose.yml` being bumped in lock-step with an rspamd image rebuild. The SKIP_OLEFY entrypoint change (commit `8408b82e`) went in simultaneously with a new published image. This PR breaks that pattern.

---

## Issue 2 — Medium: The `else` (SKIP_CLAMD=n) branch does unconditional `rm -f` on a host bind-mount

**File:** `data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh`, new lines ~118-120

**Code in PR:**
```bash
else
  rm -f "${CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF}"
fi
```
where `CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF=/etc/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf`.

**Reason:** `/etc/rspamd/override.d/` is bind-mounted from the host at `./data/conf/rspamd/override.d/` (confirmed in `docker-compose.yml` line with `./data/conf/rspamd/override.d/:/etc/rspamd/override.d:Z`). The `rm -f` runs unconditionally on every container startup when `SKIP_CLAMD != y`. If an administrator has manually placed a custom `antivirus.conf` in `override.d/` (e.g., to tune scores or point at a different scanner), it will be **silently deleted from the host filesystem** the first time `SKIP_CLAMD=n` is in effect. There is no guard checking whether the file was actually created by this script.

**Historical context:** Commit `8c8497d8` ("[Rspamd] only recreate external_services.conf file if it was deleted") was a **targeted fix** that added precisely this kind of existence guard to the analogous SKIP_OLEFY block — switching from unconditional `cat > file` to `if [[ ! -f ... ]]; then cat > file; fi` — because users had been having their hand-edited `external_services.conf` overwritten on every container restart. The PR re-introduces the exact pattern that commit fixed, but for a different file.

---

## Issue 3 — Medium: The `SKIP_CLAMD=y` write branch unconditionally overwrites `override.d/antivirus.conf` without checking prior existence

**File:** `data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh`, new lines ~110-116

**Code in PR:**
```bash
if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*servers[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*"clamd:3310";' \
       /etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf 2>/dev/null; then
  cat <<EOF > "${CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF}"
clamav {
  enabled = false;
}
EOF
fi
```

**Reason:** The guard only checks `local.d/antivirus.conf` for the default server string. It does **not** check whether `override.d/antivirus.conf` already exists. If a user has placed their own `override.d/antivirus.conf` (e.g., adjusting `symbol` weights, `log_clean`, or `max_size` for the real ClamAV they want to keep alongside `SKIP_CLAMD=n`→`y` transitions), the `cat >` redirect will **clobber it** with the minimal `clamav { enabled = false; }` content on every startup while `SKIP_CLAMD=y`. The `>` operator overwrites rather than appends, and there is no existence check.

The correct fix, consistent with the `8c8497d8` precedent, would be to guard with `if [[ ! -f "${CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF}" ]]; then` before writing, or at minimum check that the file does not already exist with non-script-generated content.

---

## Issue 4 — Minor: Stale "auto-detect" comment remains in `_modules/scripts/new_options.sh` at line 283 in current `master` (the PR does fix this, but only for the `adapt_new_options` path)

**File:** `_modules/scripts/new_options.sh`, line 283 (current master, pre-PR)

**Reason:** The PR correctly updates both `generate_config.sh` and `_modules/scripts/new_options.sh` to remove the now-false parenthetical "(Rspamd will auto-detect a missing ClamAV container)". This is fine for new installs (`generate_config.sh`) and for upgrades where `SKIP_CLAMD` is not yet present in `mailcow.conf` (`new_options.sh`). However, for **existing installations** that already have `SKIP_CLAMD` in `mailcow.conf`, neither path runs — the stale comment persists in those users' `mailcow.conf` indefinitely. This is a documentation-only concern (no functional impact), but worth noting since the comment now actively describes behavior (auto-detection) that the PR is replacing with explicit disable logic.

Score issue 2: grep guard wrong dir

```text
I now have all the information I need to score this issue. Let me understand the Rspamd configuration layering behavior. Based on standard Rspamd documentation, `override.d` settings take precedence and are loaded **after** `local.d`, so they override any settings in `local.d`.

**Analysis:**

The issue claims the guard is insufficient because:
1. Users with custom external ClamAV would configure it in `override.d/antivirus.conf` (override layer)
2. But the guard only checks `local.d/antivirus.conf` (local layer)
3. So if a user has a custom server in `override.d` and the default "clamd:3310" in `local.d`, the grep matches and the script writes `clamav { enabled = false; }` to `override.d`, disabling their custom ClamAV

**Critical examination:**

In Rspamd's configuration precedence:
- `local.d/*.conf` provides local customizations merged with defaults
- `override.d/*.conf` is loaded **after** and **overrides** local.d
- So `override.d` settings take precedence over `local.d`

The scenario claimed in the issue is:
- User has custom external ClamAV in `override.d/antivirus.conf` with `servers = "myclamav:3310"`
- Mailcow default in `local.d/antivirus.conf` still has `servers = "clamd:3310"`
- Guard checks `local.d` (finds "clamd:3310"), returns true
- Script writes `clamav { enabled = false; }` to `override.d`
- Result: ClamAV gets disabled despite user's custom config

**The problem is real:** If a user configured a custom external ClamAV by placing a server config in `override.d`, and hasn't modified `local.d`, the guard won't catch this case and will disable their custom ClamAV.

**However, this is NOT the typical user workflow:**
- Standard Rspamd practice: Users edit `local.d` for local customizations, not `override.d`
- `override.d` is typically for system-managed overrides (as mailcow uses it)
- The maintainer's concern in #6985 was about "managed mailcow setups" using `SKIP_CLAMD=y` WITH external ClamAV

**But here's the key issue:** For a truly managed external ClamAV setup (where the container is skipped and replaced with external ClamAV), users would need to change the configuration from the default somewhere. If they **never touch local.d** and only configure in `override.d`, the guard fails.

This is a **real edge case** that violates the guard's intent, but it requires a specific (unusual) configuration pattern: using `override.d` instead of the standard `local.d` for custom ClamAV config.

**Score: 75**

The guard check only verifies `local.d/antivirus.conf` but writes to `override.d/antivirus.conf`, creating a mismatch where external ClamAV configured in override.d (the non-standard layer) could be disabled, which defeats the guard's stated purpose of protecting external ClamAV setups.

You are scoring an issue found during code review of PR #7180 on repo bonitobonita24/mail_lgucalapan_ph. There are NO CLAUDE.md files in this repo.

ISSUE: The guard in `data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh` checks `local.d/antivirus.conf` for the default "clamd:3310" server string to decide whether to disable ClamAV. But a user with a custom external ClamAV would configure their custom scanner in `override.d/antivirus.conf` (the override layer), not `local.d/antivirus.conf`. So `local.d` would still contain the default "clamd:3310", the grep would match, and the script would write `clamav { enabled = false; }` to override.d — disabling their external ClamAV. The comment says "This avoids breaking custom external ClamAV setups" but the guard doesn't actually protect that case.

Historical context: Previous PR #6985 was explicitly blocked by maintainer @DerLinkman with CHANGES_REQUESTED because it would "break our managed mailcow setups" that use SKIP_CLAMD=y with an external ClamAV. PR #7180 adds this guard to address that concern, but the guard checks the wrong directory.

Score this issue on a scale of 0-100:
- 0: False positive, doesn't stand up to scrutiny, or pre-existing
- 25: Might be real but may also be false positive
- 50: Real issue but nitpick or rare in practice
- 75: Very likely real, will be hit in practice, important
- 100: Definitely real, will happen frequently, confirmed

Consider: Would users with external ClamAV configure it in override.d or local.d? Is the Rspamd config layering (local.d vs override.d) well understood? Is the maintainer's previous objection directly relevant?

Return just the score and a one-sentence justification.

Score issue 3: image tag not bumped

```text
Now I have enough information to assess this issue. Let me provide my scoring:

**ISSUE ANALYSIS:**

The core claim is that PR #7180 modifies the rspamd `docker-entrypoint.sh` to add SKIP_CLAMD logic but does NOT bump the image tag in `docker-compose.yml`, which currently references `ghcr.io/mailcow/rspamd:3.14.3-1`.

The concern is valid because:
1. The Dockerfile uses `COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh` - the entrypoint is baked into the image at build time
2. Docker image tags in the registry are immutable - an old tag will never contain new code
3. Users who `docker compose pull` after the PR merges will receive the old image without the entrypoint changes
4. The environment variable SKIP_CLAMD will be passed to the container, but the code to read it won't exist

**HOWEVER, the context matters:**

This is a **fork of mailcow**. The repository has CI/CD infrastructure (`.github/workflows/image_builds.yml`) that automatically rebuilds Docker images on pushes to master. The workflow builds all containers including `rspamd-mailcow` whenever code is pushed. This means:

1. **If the entrypoint code is added to master**, the CI/CD will automatically rebuild the rspamd image
2. **But the image tag in `docker-compose.yml` won't auto-update** - that's a separate manual step
3. **This creates a window where the tag points to a stale image** - until someone manually bumps the tag

The issue is a real concern for **deployment consistency**, but it's primarily a **release process/coordinated deployment** issue rather than a code review issue. The code change itself is not wrong, but the deployment workflow is incomplete.

**SCORE: 50**

This is a real issue but more of a procedural/release concern than a code defect, and the impact depends on whether this repo has a separate release process or whether the image rebuild and tag bump are handled as a coordinated step.

Score issue 4: maintainer blocked predecessor

```text
Now I have enough information to score this issue. Let me analyze the specific concern:

**Issue Analysis:**

1. PR #7180 adds a grep guard checking for the default "clamd:3310" in `local.d/antivirus.conf`
2. The problem identified is that users with external ClamAV configs could have them in `override.d` rather than `local.d`
3. This creates a gap: the grep guard only checks `local.d`, so if someone has their external ClamAV config in `override.d`, the guard won't find the "clamd:3310" pattern and will incorrectly disable ClamAV

**Critical facts:**

- The grep check is: `grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*servers[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*"clamd:3310";' /etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf`
- This only checks one file: `/etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf`
- rspamd loads both `local.d` and `override.d`, with `override.d` having higher priority (it can override)
- If a user has customized their external ClamAV config in `override.d/antivirus.conf` with something like `servers = "external-clamd:3310"` or any custom clamd server, the grep won't match
- The result: the code will incorrectly create `/etc/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf` with `enabled = false`, which will disable the external ClamAV integration

**Is this distinct from the grep-guard issue, or the same?**

This IS the same issue restated - it's not a separate concern. The "guard may not fully address the maintainer's concern" phrasing indicates this is explaining why the guard itself is insufficient. The core problem is the grep guard's limited scope.

**Scoring:**
- Will this definitely break external ClamAV setups? Yes, if they're in override.d or if the servers field differs
- How common is this? Moderate - some advanced users customize configs in override.d
- Will it hit in practice? Yes, for anyone using custom external ClamAV with SKIP_CLAMD=y
- Is it a real issue? Yes - it directly contradicts the maintainer's stated concern

**75 score** - This is very likely to be a real issue that will be encountered in practice by users with custom ClamAV configurations, and it's important because it directly violates the maintainer's stated requirement to preserve external ClamAV setups.

75: The grep guard only checks /etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf but cannot detect external ClamAV servers configured in override.d, causing the fix to incorrectly disable all ClamAV when any custom config exists outside local.d.

ISSUE: Previous PR #6985 attempted the same fix (disabling rspamd ClamAV integration when SKIP_CLAMD=y) and was explicitly blocked by maintainer @DerLinkman with CHANGES_REQUESTED, stating: "This will break our managed mailcow setups :) as we have a external centralized clamd which would be ignored with this setting as we set SKIP_CLAMD=y here. The general idea is not bad, but not in this way."

PR #7180 adds a guard checking for the default "clamd:3310" in local.d/antivirus.conf, but as noted in another issue, this guard may not fully address the maintainer's concern since external ClamAV configs could be in override.d rather than local.d.

- 0: False positive, doesn't stand up to scrutiny, or pre-existing
- 25: Might be real but may also be false positive
- 50: Real issue but nitpick or rare in practice
- 75: Very likely real, will be hit in practice, important
- 100: Definitely real, will happen frequently, confirmed

Consider: Is this a distinct issue from the grep-guard issue, or is it the same issue restated? Should this be merged with the guard issue or scored separately?

Return just the score and a one-sentence justification.

/home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/lgucalapan_ph/mail_lgucalapan_ph/.specstory/history/2026-04-12_14-21-02Z-review-codebase-and-load.md:The guard inspects `local.d/antivirus.conf`. However, a user with a custom external ClamAV setup would customise their scanner in `override.d/antivirus.conf` (the override layer), not `local.d/antivirus.conf` (the local layer). If a user has a custom `servers = "myclamav:3310";` in `override.d/antivirus.conf`, the grep on `local.d` would still match (because `local.d/antivirus.conf` still says `clamd:3310`), causing the PR code to write a `clamav { enabled = false; }` override — disabling their custom external scanner. The comment says the guard is there to avoid exactly this, but it does not actually protect against the override.d customisation scenario.

/home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/lgucalapan_ph/mail_lgucalapan_ph/.specstory/history/2026-04-12_14-21-02Z-review-codebase-and-load.md:ISSUE: The guard in `data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh` checks `local.d/antivirus.conf` for the default "clamd:3310" server string to decide whether to disable ClamAV. So `local.d` would still contain the default "clamd:3310", the grep would match, and the script would write `clamav { enabled = false; }` to override.d — disabling their external ClamAV. The comment says "This avoids breaking custom external ClamAV setups" but the guard doesn't actually protect that case.
/home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/lgucalapan_ph/mail_lgucalapan_ph/.specstory/history/2026-04-12_14-21-02Z-review-codebase-and-load.md:PR #7180 adds a guard checking for the default "clamd:3310" in local.d/antivirus.conf, but as noted in another issue, this guard may not fully address the maintainer's concern since external ClamAV configs could be in override.d rather than local.d.
```
</details></tool-use>

28	## Comprehensive Codebase Exploration Report
29	
30	### 1. Project Overview
31	**mailcow: dockerized** is a complete, open-source email server solution built on Docker and designed for self-hosting. It's a feature-rich mail server management platform that combines multiple specialized services into a cohesive dockerized environment.
32	
33	**Repository**: Fork of mailcow-dockerized (upstream: https://github.com/mailcow/mailcow-dockerized)
34	**Local Fork**: https://github.com/bonitobonita24/mail_lgucalapan_ph.git
35	**License**: GNU General Public License, Version 3
36	**Maintainer**: The Infrastructure Company GmbH (info@servercow.de)
37	**Latest Commit**: 8e9a9585 (Add SpecStory configuration files for project setup)
38	
39	---
40	
41	### 2. Key Directories and Their Roles
42	
43	| Directory | Purpose |
44	|-----------|---------|
45	| **`/data`** | Core application data, configuration, and web interface |
46	| **`/data/web`** | PHP-based web UI for mail server management (admin, user, domain admin interfaces) |
47	| **`/data/web/inc`** | Core PHP functions and includes (database access, authentication, mail operations) |
48	| **`/data/web/api`** | REST API with Swagger/OpenAPI documentation |
49	| **`/data/web/admin`** | Admin dashboard functionality (5 PHP files) |
50	| **`/data/Dockerfiles`** | Custom Docker images for each service (16 Dockerfiles) |
51	| **`/data/conf`** | Configuration files for all services (unbound, nginx, dovecot, postfix, rspamd, sogo, clamav, etc.) |
52	| **`/data/hooks`** | Custom hook scripts for service integration |
53	| **`/data/assets`** | Static assets (SSL certificates, templates) |
54	| **`.github/workflows`** | CI/CD automation (7 workflow files: image builds, PR checks, issue automation) |
55	| **`/_modules/scripts`** | Utility bash scripts for core functionality (4 core scripts) |
56	| **`/helper-scripts`** | Operational helpers (backup, restore, diagnostics, language updates) |
57	
58	---
59	
60	### 3. Technology Stack
61	
62	#### **Core Mail Services**
63	- **Postfix 3.7.11** - SMTP mail transport
64	- **Dovecot 2.3.21** - IMAP/POP3 with full-text search support
65	- **Postfix TLS Policy 1.8.23** - TLS policy enforcement
66	
67	#### **Backend Services**
68	- **PHP 8.2 (FPM)** - Web application runtime
69	- **MariaDB 10.11** - SQL database (mail data, configuration)
70	- **Redis 7.4.6** - Cache and session storage
71	- **Memcached** - Additional caching
72	- **Unbound 1.25** - DNS resolver (recursive)
73	
74	#### **Spam & Security**
75	- **Rspamd 3.14.3** - Advanced spam filtering (Bayesian, DKIM, SPF, DMARC)
76	- **ClamAV 1.71** - Antivirus scanning
77	- **Olefy 1.15** - OLE file scanning (Microsoft Office documents)
78	- **Netfilter** - Firewall isolation layer
79	
80	#### **Groupware & Webmail**
81	- **SOGo 5.12.5** - Unified webmail, calendar, contacts, and groupware
82	- **Nginx 1.06** - Reverse proxy and web server
83	
84	#### **Infrastructure**
85	- **Docker Compose** - Container orchestration (687 lines)
86	- **ACME/Let's Encrypt** - SSL certificate automation
87	- **Ofelia** - Scheduled job runner (cron replacement)
88	- **Watchdog** - Health monitoring and alerting
89	
90	#### **PHP Dependencies** (via Composer)
91	- **phpmailer** - Email handling
92	- **Twig 3.0** - Template engine
93	- **OAuth2 (bshaffer, league, keycloak)** - Authentication
94	- **ldaprecord** - LDAP directory integration
95	- **PHPMailParse** - MIME parsing
96	- **Bacon QR Code** - QR generation
97	- **2FA (RobThree/TwoFactorAuth)** - Two-factor authentication
98	- **Guzzle** - HTTP client
99	- **ADLdap2** - Active Directory integration
100	- **Carbon** - Date/time handling
```

```text
8602:ISSUE: The `else` branch in `data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh` runs `rm -f "${CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF}"` (where CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF=/etc/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf) unconditionally on every container start when SKIP_CLAMD is not "y". The `/etc/rspamd/override.d/` directory is bind-mounted from the host at `./data/conf/rspamd/override.d/`. If an administrator has manually placed a custom `antivirus.conf` there for tuning (e.g., adjusting scores, max_size, or pointing to an external scanner), it will be silently deleted from the host filesystem on every container restart.
```
</details></tool-use>

3819:The guard condition checks `/etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf` to decide whether ClamAV is using the default local target, but it writes the override to `CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF` which is set to `/etc/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf`. These are different files in different directories (`local.d` vs `override.d`). If a user has a custom external ClamAV setup configured in `override.d/antivirus.conf` rather than `local.d/antivirus.conf`, the guard check will pass (because `local.d` still has the default value) and the override will be written anyway, silently disabling their custom ClamAV integration. The comment says "avoids breaking custom external ClamAV setups" but the check is insufficient — it only looks at one of the two config locations where a custom setup could be defined.
3827:The `else` branch runs `rm -f "${CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF}"` every time the container starts with `SKIP_CLAMD` not set to yes. If an administrator has manually placed a legitimate `override.d/antivirus.conf` for other purposes (e.g., custom antivirus tuning unrelated to ClamAV enable/disable), it will be silently deleted on every container restart. The script unconditionally owns and destroys this file path without checking whether it was written by this script or by an admin.
4033:The guard condition checks `/etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf` to decide whether ClamAV is using the default local target, but it writes the override to `CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF` which is set to `/etc/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf`. These are different files in different directories (`local.d` vs `override.d`). If a user has a custom external ClamAV setup configured in `override.d/antivirus.conf` rather than `local.d/antivirus.conf`, the guard check will pass (because `local.d` still has the default value) and the override will be written anyway, silently disabling their custom ClamAV integration. The comment says "avoids breaking custom external ClamAV setups" but the check is insufficient — it only looks at one of the two config locations where a custom setup could be defined.
```

These are different files in different directories (`local.d` vs `override.d`). Perfect. Now I have all the context I need:

1. The OLEFY pattern (lines 84-105) shows the CORRECT behavior: `if [[ ! -f /etc/rspamd/local.d/external_services.conf ]]; then` - only writes if the file DOESN'T exist
2. Commit 8c8497d8 explicitly fixed this pattern to avoid overwriting custom admin configurations
3. The issue claims PR #7180 re-introduces the problematic pattern for antivirus.conf with SKIP_CLAMD
4. The override.d directory is bind-mounted from the host, making any deletions permanent
5. Administrators could legitimately place custom antivirus.conf for tuning (scores, max_size, external scanners)

The pattern is identical to what was fixed for OLEFY - this is a real issue that demonstrates a clear understanding of the problem space.

**75: Very likely real, will be hit in practice, important**

The historical precedent (commit 8c8497d8) provides strong evidence that the project maintainers already recognized this exact pattern as problematic, the override.d directory being bind-mounted makes the consequences permanent and destructive, and administrators have legitimate use cases for custom antivirus configurations.

Perfect, now let me check the actual PR #7180 to see what it's modifying in the entrypoint:

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-12 14:42:11Z)**_

I now have all the information I need to score this issue. Let me understand the Rspamd configuration layering behavior. Based on standard Rspamd documentation, `override.d` settings take precedence and are loaded **after** `local.d`, so they override any settings in `local.d`.

**Analysis:**

The issue claims the guard is insufficient because:
1. Users with custom external ClamAV would configure it in `override.d/antivirus.conf` (override layer)
2. But the guard only checks `local.d/antivirus.conf` (local layer)
3. So if a user has a custom server in `override.d` and the default "clamd:3310" in `local.d`, the grep matches and the script writes `clamav { enabled = false; }` to `override.d`, disabling their custom ClamAV

**Critical examination:**

In Rspamd's configuration precedence:
- `local.d/*.conf` provides local customizations merged with defaults
- `override.d/*.conf` is loaded **after** and **overrides** local.d
- So `override.d` settings take precedence over `local.d`

The scenario claimed in the issue is:
- User has custom external ClamAV in `override.d/antivirus.conf` with `servers = "myclamav:3310"`
- Mailcow default in `local.d/antivirus.conf` still has `servers = "clamd:3310"`
- Guard checks `local.d` (finds "clamd:3310"), returns true
- Script writes `clamav { enabled = false; }` to `override.d`
- Result: ClamAV gets disabled despite user's custom config

**The problem is real:** If a user configured a custom external ClamAV by placing a server config in `override.d`, and hasn't modified `local.d`, the guard won't catch this case and will disable their custom ClamAV.

**However, this is NOT the typical user workflow:**
- Standard Rspamd practice: Users edit `local.d` for local customizations, not `override.d`
- `override.d` is typically for system-managed overrides (as mailcow uses it)
- The maintainer's concern in #6985 was about "managed mailcow setups" using `SKIP_CLAMD=y` WITH external ClamAV

**But here's the key issue:** For a truly managed external ClamAV setup (where the container is skipped and replaced with external ClamAV), users would need to change the configuration from the default somewhere. If they **never touch local.d** and only configure in `override.d`, the guard fails.

This is a **real edge case** that violates the guard's intent, but it requires a specific (unusual) configuration pattern: using `override.d` instead of the standard `local.d` for custom ClamAV config.

**Score: 75**

The guard check only verifies `local.d/antivirus.conf` but writes to `override.d/antivirus.conf`, creating a mismatch where external ClamAV configured in override.d (the non-standard layer) could be disabled, which defeats the guard's stated purpose of protecting external ClamAV setups.

Now let me understand the exact issue. The concern is:

1. The rspamd Dockerfile bakes the entrypoint into the image at build time (line 34: `COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh`)
2. PR #7180 modifies the entrypoint source but doesn't bump the image tag
3. The docker-compose.yml points to `ghcr.io/mailcow/rspamd:3.14.3-1`
4. When users `docker compose pull`, they'll get the old image

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-12 14:42:57Z)**_

Now I have enough information to assess this issue. Let me provide my scoring:

**ISSUE ANALYSIS:**

The core claim is that PR #7180 modifies the rspamd `docker-entrypoint.sh` to add SKIP_CLAMD logic but does NOT bump the image tag in `docker-compose.yml`, which currently references `ghcr.io/mailcow/rspamd:3.14.3-1`.

The concern is valid because:
1. The Dockerfile uses `COPY docker-entrypoint.sh /docker-entrypoint.sh` - the entrypoint is baked into the image at build time
2. Docker image tags in the registry are immutable - an old tag will never contain new code
3. Users who `docker compose pull` after the PR merges will receive the old image without the entrypoint changes
4. The environment variable SKIP_CLAMD will be passed to the container, but the code to read it won't exist

**HOWEVER, the context matters:**

This is a **fork of mailcow**. The repository has CI/CD infrastructure (`.github/workflows/image_builds.yml`) that automatically rebuilds Docker images on pushes to master. The workflow builds all containers including `rspamd-mailcow` whenever code is pushed. This means:

1. **If the entrypoint code is added to master**, the CI/CD will automatically rebuild the rspamd image
2. **But the image tag in `docker-compose.yml` won't auto-update** - that's a separate manual step
3. **This creates a window where the tag points to a stale image** - until someone manually bumps the tag

The issue is a real concern for **deployment consistency**, but it's primarily a **release process/coordinated deployment** issue rather than a code review issue. The code change itself is not wrong, but the deployment workflow is incomplete.

**SCORE: 50**

This is a real issue but more of a procedural/release concern than a code defect, and the impact depends on whether this repo has a separate release process or whether the image rebuild and tag bump are handled as a coordinated step.

```text
   - When SKIP_CLAMD=y, creates `/etc/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf` with `clamav { enabled = false; }` to disable the ClamAV module
   - Only disables if the default local Mailcow ClamAV target (clamd:3310) is configured, preserving custom external ClamAV setups
   - Removes the config file when SKIP_CLAMD is not set

3. **docker-compose.yml**
   - Passes SKIP_CLAMD environment variable to rspamd-mailcow container (defaults to 'n')

4. **generate_config.sh**
   - Updated comment to remove outdated text about auto-detection

**Key Changes:**
- The rspamd entrypoint now reads SKIP_CLAMD from environment and actively disables ClamAV in the config rather than relying on Rspamd's auto-detection
- Changes are backward compatible and only affect the default setup (custom ClamAV servers are preserved)
- Testing confirmed the fix eliminates timeout warnings and processes emails without ClamAV errors

---

**Full Diff:**
```diff
diff --git a/_modules/scripts/new_options.sh b/_modules/scripts/new_options.sh
index 2c39c8a7c2..c509c91b46 100644
--- a/_modules/scripts/new_options.sh
+++ b/_modules/scripts/new_options.sh
@@ -280,7 +280,7 @@ adapt_new_options() {
             echo ENABLE_IPV6=${IPV6_BOOL} >> mailcow.conf
             ;;
         SKIP_CLAMD)
-            echo '# Skip ClamAV (clamd-mailcow) anti-virus (Rspamd will auto-detect a missing ClamAV container) - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
+            echo '# Skip ClamAV (clamd-mailcow) anti-virus - y/n' >> mailcow.conf
             echo 'SKIP_CLAMD=n' >> mailcow.conf
             ;;
         SKIP_OLEFY)
diff --git a/data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh b/data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh
index a764725cb7..d0876d09dd 100755
--- a/data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh
+++ b/data/Dockerfiles/rspamd/docker-entrypoint.sh
@@ -104,6 +104,21 @@ EOF
   fi
 fi
 
+CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF=/etc/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf
+if [[ "${SKIP_CLAMD}" =~ ^([yY][eE][sS]|[yY])+$ ]]; then
+  # Only auto-disable when antivirus.conf still points to the default local Mailcow
+  # ClamAV target. This avoids breaking custom external ClamAV setups.
+  if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*servers[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*"clamd:3310";' /etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf 2>/dev/null; then
+    cat <<EOF > "${CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF}"
+clamav {
+  enabled = false;
+}
+EOF
+  fi
+else
+  rm -f "${CLAMD_AUTO_DISABLE_CONF}"
+fi
+
 # Provide additional lua modules
 ln -s /usr/lib/$(uname -m)-linux-gnu/liblua5.1-cjson.so.0.0.0 /usr/lib/rspamd/cjson.so

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-12 14:43:45Z)**_

Now I have enough information to score this issue. Let me analyze the specific concern:

**Issue Analysis:**

1. PR #7180 adds a grep guard checking for the default "clamd:3310" in `local.d/antivirus.conf`
2. The problem identified is that users with external ClamAV configs could have them in `override.d` rather than `local.d`
3. This creates a gap: the grep guard only checks `local.d`, so if someone has their external ClamAV config in `override.d`, the guard won't find the "clamd:3310" pattern and will incorrectly disable ClamAV

**Critical facts:**

- The grep check is: `grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*servers[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*"clamd:3310";' /etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf`
- This only checks one file: `/etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf`
- rspamd loads both `local.d` and `override.d`, with `override.d` having higher priority (it can override)
- If a user has customized their external ClamAV config in `override.d/antivirus.conf` with something like `servers = "external-clamd:3310"` or any custom clamd server, the grep won't match
- The result: the code will incorrectly create `/etc/rspamd/override.d/antivirus.conf` with `enabled = false`, which will disable the external ClamAV integration

**Is this distinct from the grep-guard issue, or the same?**

This IS the same issue restated - it's not a separate concern. The "guard may not fully address the maintainer's concern" phrasing indicates this is explaining why the guard itself is insufficient. The core problem is the grep guard's limited scope.

**Scoring:**
- Will this definitely break external ClamAV setups? Yes, if they're in override.d or if the servers field differs
- How common is this? Moderate - some advanced users customize configs in override.d
- Will it hit in practice? Yes, for anyone using custom external ClamAV with SKIP_CLAMD=y
- Is it a real issue? Yes - it directly contradicts the maintainer's stated concern

**75 score** - This is very likely to be a real issue that will be encountered in practice by users with custom ClamAV configurations, and it's important because it directly violates the maintainer's stated requirement to preserve external ClamAV setups.

75: The grep guard only checks /etc/rspamd/local.d/antivirus.conf but cannot detect external ClamAV servers configured in override.d, causing the fix to incorrectly disable all ClamAV when any custom config exists outside local.d.
